behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all file paths, field names (network, javaScript, target, log), types (Lazy<V143Network> etc.), and property patterns that the tests check.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction provides the exact code to write, including field declarations, constructor initialization lines, and property accessor expressions verbatim — reducing the task to copy-paste.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests enforce exact field names (e.g. 'private readonly Lazy<V143Network> network') and property syntax 'Network => this.network.Value', rejecting alternatives like caching via static field or using a different private name.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests are regex text searches on .cs files (read_file + re.search); no code is compiled or executed to verify the lazy behavior actually holds.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each fail_to_pass test asserts for patterns not present in the base code (Lazy<> fields, .Value returns), so a stub cannot satisfy them without making the structural changes.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Fields, constructor initialization, property accessors, and all three versions are tested as required by instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with header comments for FAIL-TO-PASS and PASS-TO-PASS and named descriptively.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a C# code edit, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths like dotnet/src/webdriver/DevTools/v143/V143Domains.cs and identifier names are consistent across instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction specifies exact implementation code blocks (constructor lambdas and property accessors), far beyond naming the affected module.
